message:
fix(http): use naive UTC datetimes for SQLite compatibility

SQLite stores naive datetime values, but the code was using
datetime.now(timezone.utc) which creates timezone-aware objects.
This caused comparison issues with existing naive timestamps in the DB.

Fixed 4 locations by adding .replace(tzinfo=None):
- mark_messages_read endpoint (line 1782)
- mark_all_read endpoint (line 1852)
- overseer agent creation (line 2277)
- overseer message creation (line 2298)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1779,7 +1779,8 @@ async def mark_selected_messages_read(project: str, agent: str, request: Request
                     aid = int(arow[0])
 
                     # Mark specific messages as read
-                    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
+                    # Use naive UTC datetime for SQLite compatibility
+                    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(tzinfo=None)
 
                     # Use IN clause with parameter binding
                     placeholders = ','.join([f':mid{i}' for i in range(len(message_ids))])
@@ -1849,7 +1850,8 @@ async def mark_all_messages_read(project: str, agent: str) -> JSONResponse:
                     aid = int(arow[0])
 
                     # Mark all unread messages as read
-                    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
+                    # Use naive UTC datetime for SQLite compatibility
+                    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(tzinfo=None)
                     result = await session.execute(
                         text(
                             """

                                 "task": "Human operator providing guidance and oversight to agents",
                                 "policy": "open",
                                 "attachments_policy": "auto",
-                                "ts": datetime.now(timezone.utc),
+                                # Use naive UTC datetime for SQLite compatibility
+                                "ts": datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(tzinfo=None),
                             },
                         )
                         # Don't commit yet - wait until message is successfully created and written to Git

                     # Insert message into database
                     message_id = None

+                    # Use naive UTC datetime for SQLite compatibility
